name: best-rust-patterns
description: Production-grade Rust skills — actionbook/rust-skills enhanced with source-cited patterns reverse-engineered from real Rust codebases.
author: LeventySeven

enhancements.method: Patterns reverse-engineered from production Rust source, mapped to skill gaps, applied as additive source-cited inserts, then adversarially verified against the cited source.

m01-ownership: PhantomData variance marker table (Zed gpui Entity<T>)
m03-mutability: mem::take / mem::swap to dodge borrow conflicts (Zed/LanceDB)
m04-zero-cost: GAT + dynamic() escape hatch dissolving static-vs-dyn dichotomy (LanceDB)
m06-error-handling: context! macro w/ file!()/line!() on a typed enum (DataFusion)
m10-performance: cheap O(1) pre-filter before expensive compare (Zed)
m11-ecosystem: zero-dep base crate for compile cost (LakeSail)
m15-anti-pattern: best lock is no lock — actor alternative (LakeSail)
domain-ml: hand-written Default with per-param paper cites (LanceDB)
domain-embedded: no_std drops transcendental float math -> libm (turbopuffer)

correctness_fixes: m01-ownership: corrected PhantomData variance table (covariant/invariant/contravariant) — upstream teardown had it wrong on 3/5 rows
m07-concurrency: corrected LakeSail actor field names; marked illustrative type params
m12-lifecycle: removed two assertions made beyond the cited source
domain-cli: separated verbatim source comments from illustrative skeleton
rust-daily: removed nonexistent agent-browser --limit flag from 6 commands
